assertion_results.text: Step 0 detects the absence of the Deployment Context column in the Source Repositories table and defaults all repositories to upstream (§1.78)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md lines 35-37 contain a Deployment Context section stating: '**upstream** (default -- Deployment Context column is absent from the Source Repositories table in CLAUDE.md; all repositories default to `upstream` per backward-compatibility rule).' This explicitly detects the absence and defaults to upstream.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task descriptions do NOT include a Coordination Guidance subsection — the section is omitted entirely when no Deployment Context column exists
assertion_results.evidence: All four remediation task descriptions in remediation.md (Task 1 lines 32-76, Task 2 lines 84-122, Task 3 lines 134-183, Task 4 lines 189-233) contain only these subsections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, Dependencies. No 'Coordination Guidance' subsection appears anywhere in the remediation output.

assertion_results.text: The triage proceeds normally through all steps without errors from the missing Deployment Context column
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md (Step 1) completes successfully with all sections populated: CVE data, Stream Scope, Ecosystem, Deployment Context (defaulted), Version Impact Table (7 versions analyzed), Upstream Fix Status, Affects Versions Correction, and Cross-Stream Impact. remediation.md (Step 8) produces four complete remediation tasks across two streams (2.2.x and 2.1.x). No error messages, warnings, or incomplete sections appear in either output file.

assertion_results.text: Backward compatibility is maintained — existing eval behavior is unaffected by the absence of the Deployment Context column
assertion_results.evidence: The output explicitly invokes backward compatibility (data-extraction.md line 37: 'per backward-compatibility rule'). All standard triage behaviors function correctly: version impact analysis identifies affected/unaffected versions, upstream fix status is checked per stream, Affects Versions is corrected from incorrect 2.0.0 to 2.2.0/2.2.1/2.2.2, cross-stream impact triggers Case B for 2.1.x, and remediation produces both standard (Depend link) and preemptive (Related link, security-preemptive label) tasks. The absence of the column causes no degradation in any triage capability.
